segments.goal: Map a linear system into one manipulable matrix object.
beats: Separate coefficient block and constants column.
Explain vertical bar notation and what must remain aligned.
voiceover: Augmented matrices keep equation structure while making elimination systematic.
Every row operation must preserve solution equivalence.
segments.animation: system-to-matrix morph with coefficient/constant color coding

segments.goal: State and justify the row-equivalence principle.
beats: State equivalence of solution sets under these operations.
Clarify nonzero scalar requirement for row scaling.
voiceover: Elementary operations change representation, not the solution set.
That invariant is the reason Gaussian elimination is mathematically valid.

segments.goal: Execute two row operations and interpret each move.
beats: Choose first pivot in column one.
Eliminate below-pivot entries using row replacement.
Interpret resulting row as simplified equation.
voiceover: Name the target zero first, then select the row operation.
After each operation, read the new row as an equation to avoid blind manipulation.
segments.animation: row-operation stepper with before/after matrix panes

segments.goal: Prevent common algebraic and structural mistakes.
beats: Checkpoint: constants column transformed together with coefficient columns.
Checkpoint: row scaling never uses zero multiplier.
Checkpoint: each operation documented in notation R_i←...
voiceover: Most elimination errors are bookkeeping errors, not concept errors.
Consistent operation notation makes audit and QA possible.
